channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Compared 5 Heatmap Tools. They All Have the Same Blind Spot
item.description: "Will adding a heatmap tool grow our revenue?" It's the most common question I hear from ecommerce operators. The honest answer turned out to be more interesting than I expected. I compared five...

item.title: Calmoro
item.description: I built Calmoro — an adaptive workday OS for remote workers. It helps you plan your day, predict where work might drift, recover with small actions, and shut down intentionally. The core idea :...

item.title: Is it English not Technical Skill Stopping You From Interviewing?
item.description: You're a technical expert but interviews feel so daunting. Especially for international companies where you need English. The last time you applied you drilled leetcode, prepared to talk about a...

item.title: Free Project Management Tools I Actually Use to Run Volunteer Teams (Asana Alternatives That Don't Require a Budget)
item.description: TL;DR: I ran a volunteer onboarding project for a mid-sized nonprofit and we blew past Asana's free tier limit inside the first weekend of signups. The cap is 15 members — not 15 active users, not...

item.title: Patrones de acceso seguro a S3 🌐
item.description: Este análisis se propone clarificar algunos casos de uso donde se requiere servir contenido de S3 de manera segura, identificando las brechas de seguridad a las que debemos enfrentarnos y explorar...

item.title: 🚀 Introducing Laravel Shortcodes – Bring WordPress-like Shortcodes to Laravel
item.description: I’ve built a Laravel package that introduces shortcode functionality, allowing developers to handle dynamic content in a more flexible and reusable way within PHP applications. It’s designed for...

item.title: Masky.js: A Lightweight Alternative to Inputmask, Cleave.js, and IMask
item.description: Finding the right input masking library can be tricky. There are many options, each with its pros and cons. Some are feature-packed but heavy, while others are lightweight but miss critical...

item.title: The Consequences of Agentic AI
item.description: Customer support agents are hallucinating policies. Coding agents are deleting production resources. Dependency supply chains are getting poisoned. Agent prototypes are failing in QA due to...

item.title: Our Auditor Asked How We Prove Logs Aren
item.description: We were 4 months into our SOC 2 audit when the auditor asked a question that stopped us cold: "How do you prove that your audit logs haven't been modified after the fact?" We looked at each other....

item.title: The Man Behind AlphaGo Thinks AI Is Taking the Wrong Path
item.description: David Silver has a new billion-dollar company that aims to build AI “superlearners.”

item.title: That UL safety logo is a lot more complicated than it looks
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Jennifer Scanlon, who is the CEO of UL Solutions. That’s Underwriters Laboratories – you know, the UL logo listed on all your electronics? That symbol means it’s been...

item.title: Amazon snaps up Oprah Winfrey’s podcast
item.description: Oprah Winfrey's video podcast, Book Club, and Favorite Things are headed to Amazon, according to reports from The New York Times and Variety. Starting in July, The Oprah Podcast will get new...

item.title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
item.description: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

item.title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
item.description: The phone could go in mass production in 2028, an analyst says.

item.title: Rebuilding the data stack for AI
item.description: Artificial intelligence may be dominating boardroom agendas, but many enterprises are discovering that the biggest obstacle to meaningful adoption is the state of their data. While consumer-facing...

item.title: The Download: DeepSeek’s latest AI breakthrough, and the race to build world models
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters On Friday,...

item.title: Govee’s multicolor ceiling light doubles as a low-res screen
item.description: The smart lighting company is having a busy month. After releasing its first solar-powered lights, a cordless table lamp, and an updated LED light wall over the past few weeks, Govee has announced...

item.title: Spotify is partnering with Peloton for guided workouts
item.description: Spotify has dabbled in customized running playlists, but now it's diving more firmly into the fitness space with curated playlists and content from creators like Yoga with Kassandra, Sweaty...

item.title: Mythos Changed the Math on Vulnerability Discovery. Most Teams Aren't Ready for the Remediation Side
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude Mythos Preview has dominated security discussions since its April 7 announcement. Early reporting describes a powerful cybersecurity-focused AI system capable of identifying...

item.title: PhantomCore Exploits TrueConf Vulnerabilities to Breach Russian Networks
item.description: A pro-Ukrainian hacktivist group called PhantomCore has been attributed to attacks actively targeting servers running TrueConf video conferencing software in Russia since September 2025. That's...

item.title: Is this Samsung’s upcoming wide foldable?
item.description: The wide foldable phone that Samsung is reportedly developing is expected to arrive later this year, and now we may have some idea of what it will look like. Leaker and journalist Sonny Dickson...

item.title: Researchers Uncover 73 Fake VS Code Extensions Delivering GlassWorm v2 Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged dozens of Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extensions on the Open VSX repository that are linked to a persistent information-stealing campaign dubbed...

item.title: Medieval Encrypted Letter Decoded
item.description: Sent by a Spanish diplomat. Apparently people have been working on it since it was rediscovered in 1860.

item.title: The AI-designed car is taking shape
item.description: The auto design world is full of advanced 3D visualization tools and VR sculpting platforms, but your average new car still enters the world as a sketch. Those sketches traditionally see endless...

item.title: This touchscreen mouse is my over-engineering nightmare
item.description: Turtle Beach's latest collection of PC peripherals are so focused around touchscreen displays that the company even slapped one on a gaming mouse. The $160 wireless Command Series MC7 features a...

item.title: Meta inks deal for solar power at night, beamed from space
item.description: Overview Energy's first contract with Meta is a small step toward a future of space-based solar power.

item.title: Fake CAPTCHA IRSF Scam and 120 Keitaro Campaigns Drive Global SMS, Crypto Fraud
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a telecommunications fraud campaign that uses fake CAPTCHA verification tricks to dupe unsuspecting users into sending international text...

item.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership
item.description: OpenAI and Microsoft announce an amended agreement that simplifies the partnership, adds long-term clarity, and supports continued AI innovation at scale.
